title: chaerophyllum lineare
intro: Chaerophyllum lineare, also known as linear chervil or wormseed fern, is a perennial herbaceous plant that belongs to the carrot family and is native to Europe and Asia. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties. The health benefits of Chaerophyllum lineare include relief from digestive issues, such as flatulence and bloating, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate respiratory problems like coughs and colds. Chaerophyllum lineare can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and it is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
